name: Authzed (SpiceDB Cloud) API
what_it_does: Managed Zanzibar-style authorization service (cloud-hosted SpiceDB) with gRPC/REST API — enabling fine-grained relationship-based access control (ReBAC) at Google-scale, with schema definition language, relationship management, and permission check API.
use_cases: Google Docs-style sharing permissions (owner/editor/viewer) with relationship inheritance
Fine-grained permission checks at request time for multi-tenant applications
Externalizing authorization from application code to dedicated authorization service
Building permission systems that inherit access through organizational hierarchies
Consistent authorization with zedtokens (causality tokens) for distributed systems
not_for: Simple RBAC without relationship hierarchies (use Casbin or plain role checks)
Teams without engineering investment in ReBAC schema design
best_when: An application needs Google-scale relationship-based authorization with inheritance, delegation, and consistent distributed permission checks.
avoid_when: Simple role-based access is sufficient, or team lacks resources to invest in ReBAC schema modeling.

auth.notes: API tokens with permission level (read-only, read-write, admin). Token scopes per permission set. gRPC bearer token auth. REST via gRPC-gateway. Separate tokens per permission schema tenant.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: serverless tier with limited operations/month
pricing.paid_tiers: Serverless from usage-based; Dedicated from $500/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: SpiceDB is Apache 2.0 open source — free to self-host. Authzed Cloud is managed SpiceDB. Strong funding and adoption by GitHub, Airbnb, etc. US-based. Serverless tier for development and small-scale production. Dedicated instances for performance-sensitive production.

performance.rate_limits: Serverless tier: operations per second limited; Dedicated: configurable

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC primary with REST gateway. Multi-language SDKs (6 languages). Schema authoring in Authzed Schema Language (ASL). Zedtokens for causality tracking — critical for consistency. Good documentation with playground. Permission check API returns ALLOW/DENY with latency suitable for hot path.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Permission checks fully idempotent reads. WriteRelationships idempotent (touch semantics). DeleteRelationships idempotent. Schema writes idempotent if compatible.
known_agent_gotchas: Schema modeling requires deep understanding of ReBAC — significant upfront investment
Zedtokens required for causal consistency — must track and pass tokens across distributed writes
Schema migrations must be backwards-compatible — breaking changes require careful planning
gRPC primary interface — REST gateway adds latency; use gRPC SDK in production

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source SpiceDB — auditable. SOC2 Type II certified (Authzed Cloud). GDPR compliant. TLS enforced. Token scopes for permission level. Multi-tenant isolation. US and EU data residency. Used by GitHub — production-proven security.
